title: Box Frame Rectangle Side Table
summary: A low 1x8 tabletop set on two open 2x2 box frames, finished in a two-tone look with the frame stained or painted before the top ever touches it.
description:
The frame goes together and dries flat before the top is ever involved — build both 2x2 box frames, let them cure on a flat, level surface, then fill and sand the screw holes. That gap between frame and top is also the easiest point to get a two-tone finish: stain or paint the frame at this stage and leave its top edges raw, since bare wood is what the glue needs to bond the tabletop down in the final step.

Measure the actual width of your two joined 1x8 tabletop boards before cutting the remaining 2x2 frame pieces — the cut list assumes a particular combined width, and real boards can run slightly narrow or wide. The frame length numbers below already account for that with a "3" less than tabletop width" rule; use your own measurement, not the number on paper, if they don't match.

cutList.note: 3" less than the actual combined width of the tabletop boards.

steps.title: Cut and check the tabletop boards
steps.body: Cut the tabletop boards, lay them together, and measure the combined width. If it doesn't match the cut list, adjust the frame cross-rail lengths to 3" less than your actual measured width. If using a pocket hole jig, join the two 1x8 boards together first.

steps.title: Build the two end frames
steps.body: Build two identical end frames from the legs and long rails as shown, using 2-1/2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Join the frames
steps.body: Cut the remaining cross rails 3" shorter than the width of the tabletop and attach to assemble the full frame. Let it dry on a flat, level surface, fill the screw holes with wood filler, let dry, and sand well. This is a good stage to apply a contrasting finish to the frame while leaving its top edges raw.

steps.title: Attach the tabletop
steps.body: Apply glue and set the tabletop on the frame. Attach with 1-1/4" brad nails or 1-5/8" trim screws. Fill any holes with wood filler, sand smooth once dry, and finish the tabletop.

images.alt: A low rectangular side table with a 1x8 top on an open 2x2 box frame
